LAW AND MOTION 302, REAL-PARTY-IN-INTEREST MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA, MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA XIV'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED; DEFENDANTS ARE TO ANSWER WITHIN 10 DAYS OF SERVICE OF THE ORDER. THE DEMURRER RAISES MULTIPLE ISSUES THAT ARE BEST ADDRESSED BY SUBSEQUENT MOTIONS BASED ON ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. THE PETITION STATES SUFFICIENT FACTS THAT COUNTER RESPONDENTS' CONTENTIONS. -THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF LAKRITZ' EXHIBITS A, A-1, A-2, C, C-1 AND D. -LACK OF STANDING IS BASED ON THE FAILURE TO OBJECT TO CEQA FINDINGS. HOWEVER, THE COMPLAINT PLEADS LACK OF NOTICE. (PARAGRAPHS 15-19.) TAKING INTO ACCOUNT THE DOCUMENTS JUDICIALLY NOTICED, THE COURT CANNOT DECIDE, AS A MATTER OF LAW, THAT PETITIONER HAD NOTICE THAT THE SIDEWALK WOULD BE EXTENDED 5 FEET INTO JESSIE STREET. MOREOVER, THE ARGUMENT MADE IN SUPPORT OF A LACK OF STANDING IS CONTRARY TO THE ARGUMENT MADE IN CHALLENGING THE NUISANCE CLAIM. -EXHAUSTION OF ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES IS BASED ON NOTICE AND FOR THE SAME REASON, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. -IT APPEARS THAT THE APPLICABLE LIMITATIONS PERIOD IS 180 DAYS. HOWEVER, THE STATUTE EITHER DID NOT BEGIN TO RUN AND/OR WAS TOLLED GIVEN THE UNCERTAINTY OF THE PROPOSAL TO REDUCE THE WIDTH OF JESSIE STREET. (PARAGRAPHS 15-20.) -THE PETITION ALLEGES IMMINENT HARM AND AN INTENT TO IGNORE CITY REGULATIONS; THOSE ALLEGATIONS NEGATE THE RESPONDENTS' CONTENTIONS THAT THE NUISANCE CLAIMS FAIL. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: PEPPINA THOMPSON, CSR #7433

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Jul-13-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Set For Hearing On Friday, July 13, 2007, Line 8. Real-Party-In-Interest Mercy Housing California, Mercy Housing California Xiv's Demurrer To Petition For Writ Of Mandate/ Prohibition/ Certification:
Appearances: Jonathan R. Bass, Esquire And Howard A Slavitt, Esquire (Coblentz Patch Duffy & Bass, 415.772.5726) Appearing For Petitioner. Thomas A. Lakritz, Deputy City Attorney (Office Of The City Attorney, 415.554.6547) Appearing For Respondent. Steven L. Vettel, Esquire (Farella Braun & Martell, 415.954.4400) Appearing For Real-Party-In-Interest Mercy Housing.
Ruling: Argued And The Court Adopted Its Tentative Ruling:
The Demurrer Is Overruled; Defendants Are To Answer Within 10 Days Of Service Of The Order. The Demurrer Raises Multiple Issues That Are Best Addressed By Subsequent Motions Based On Admissible Evidence. The Petition States Sufficient Facts That Counter Respondents' Contentions.
- The Court Takes Judicial Notice Of Lakritz' Exhibits A, A-1, A-2, C, C-1 And D.
- Lack Of Standing Is Based On The Failure To Object To Ceqa Findings. However, The Complaint Pleads Lack Of Notice. (Paragraphs 15-19.) Taking Into Account The Documents Judicially Noticed, The Court Cannot Decide, As A Matter Of Law, That Petitioner Had Notice That The Sidewalk Would Be Extended 5 Feet Into Jessie Street. Moreover, The Argument Made In Support Of A Lack Of Standing Is Contrary To The Argument Made In Challenging The Nuisance Claim.
- Exhaustion Of Administrative Remedies Is Based On Notice And For The Same Reason, The Demurrer Is Overruled.
- It Appears That The Applicable Limitations Period Is 180 Days. However, The Statute Either Did Not Begin To Run And/Or Was Tolled Given The Uncertainty Of The Proposal To Reduce The Width Of Jessie Street. (Paragraphs 15-20.)
- The Petition Alleges Imminent Harm And An Intent To Ignore City Regulations; Those Allegations Negate The Respondents' Contentions That The Nuisance Claims Fail. (302/Pjm/Kd) Judge: Patrick
